title: evathons
wikitext:
[]: evathons             Quote Post by misatolover45 » Tue Aug 15, 2006 3:14 pm im going to visit a friend in a week and we are planning on having an evathon, watching all 26 episodes and eoe in one day. has anyone done this any have any thoughts about it?

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Aug 15, 2006 3:20 pm Yes, and you have to add D&R to that list for it to be a true Evathon. And it really isn't anything special, most of the people on this board have probably done it at one time or another(I think there might even be a thread about this already but I am not sure). The real question is have they done it twice?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 15, 2006 3:39 pm Never. Not even tempted.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Aug 15, 2006 3:42 pm I've done it twice, although both times I started late in the day and took an overnight break to sleep in the middle of it. (Also did Cowboy Bebop once). You're not going to feel fantastic at the end.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Aug 15, 2006 3:56 pm I've never actually had a marathon. I've considered having ones for Star Wars and Lord of the Rings, but I never summoned up the nerve to actually do it, plus I'd need to have some peeps over for it to be fun.

[]:             Quote Post by badc0de » Tue Aug 15, 2006 4:54 pm I've never done one, but several times I was about to do one... with my brother... tied up and with matches between his eyelids :twisted: Some day I will proselytize him to NGE Or I should get a video projector(*) and a big screen, than I can watch it outside and proselytize our whole neighborhood. XD (*) Damn pseudo-anglicisms! I've almost written "beamer" which is the German word for video projector. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Tue Aug 15, 2006 5:25 pm I have these all of the time, it drains me though. I once watched evangelion for 3 days w/o eating or sleeping. it took awhile since i watched the whole series then with the directors cut then i read the manga and looked through my artbooks. I was exausted.

[]:             Quote Post by treeoflife » Tue Aug 15, 2006 5:36 pm Actually, since Eva kind of takes you for a mindfuck, I don't think I could have an Evathon. I don't want to have dreams of a creepy Lillith-Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Aug 15, 2006 5:41 pm The first time I watched it, I watched all 26 episodes in one sitting (I couldn't stop). Immediately afterwards, I ordered the EoE DVD. Since then, I haven't the drive to watch all 26 episodes + movies in one sitting because I already know what's going to happen next.

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Tue Aug 15, 2006 7:27 pm Never try it...., sounds too Otaku for me?!

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Tue Aug 15, 2006 7:29 pm reagen wrote: Never try it...., sounds too Otaku for me?!

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Tue Aug 15, 2006 7:32 pm I just like it there.., I guess

[]: Re: evathons             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Aug 15, 2006 7:44 pm misatolover45 wrote: im going to visit a friend in a week and we are planning on having an evathon, watching all 26 episodes and eoe in one day. has anyone done this any have any thoughts about it?

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Tue Aug 15, 2006 7:59 pm I did it once, took me all weekend to do it but it was worth it. Also, if you drink nothing but red bull and eat nothing it will help. I was told this back around a year+ ago when I made a thread for this same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Tue Aug 15, 2006 8:15 pm Such concentrated depression and thought is NOT, I repeat NOT, advised.

[]:             Quote Post by misatolover45 » Tue Aug 15, 2006 9:58 pm i had another friend who did it more than once and says its an experience. i had a lord of the rings marathon last year and i loved it, although eva is a lot more difficult to watch than lotr...

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Wed Aug 16, 2006 6:17 am The most i've ever done is about up to Episode 12 when my mate was round, this was about 2-3 days after I had first watched it myself. I don't think i'd ever go for all 26 Episodes plus the movie in one day though, its too depressing if I was going to do something like that it'd have to be something more upbeat then Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Wed Aug 16, 2006 7:18 am I really never thought about it before. After my exams this year I had a long vacation, so I watched Eva again. I watched 2-4 episodes a day, and finished the whole series including EoE in merely two weeks or so. That was already intense enough for me, an Evathon would have caused too much unrest inside my mind.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Aug 16, 2006 1:52 pm I did an Evathon when I first got the Platinum thinpak. I have no regrets. None, I say!

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Aug 16, 2006 4:08 pm I always watch Death before EoE, and if I'm watching any of the series I tend to just watch the entire DVD, but I've never really done any more than that.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Tue Aug 15, 2006 3:20 pm Yes, and you have to add D&R to that list for it to be a tru
